title: Lamb Paella with Irish Twist
description: A twist on the classic Spanish dish, this lamb paella incorporates Irish ingredients for a unique flavor profile. The citrusy note from the lemon pairs perfectly with the rich flavor of lamb.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of butter in a large paella pan over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb shoulder and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the Irish smoked bacon and cook until crispy, about 3 minutes.
Add the garlic and smoked paprika and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chicken broth, saffron rice, lemon zest, salt, and black pepper. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes.
Remove the lid and continue cooking for an additional 10 minutes, or until the rice is tender and the liquid has been absorbed.
Remove from heat and let rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Slice the lamb against the grain and serve with the paella.
